title: daphne mezereum subsp. mezereum
intro: Daphne mezereum subsp. mezereum, also known as the spurge laurel, is a perennial shrub belonging to the Thymelaeaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions such as eczema, wounds, and ulcers due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The spurge laurel is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Daphne mezereum subsp. mezereum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to alleviate skin irritations and promote overall skin health.
